ksa_text: Knowledge of data breach notification requirements across applicable jurisdictions, including notification timelines, content requirements, authority versus data subject notification obligations, and breach severity assessment criteria.

eu_ai_act.top_element_label: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

onet.top_element_label: Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

top_anchors.element_label: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Regulatory Compliance falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of Analytics. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Regulatory Compliance truly out-of-scope for the Director of Analytics? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Regulatory Compliance as a core or recommended competency for the Director of Analytics. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Regulatory Compliance falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of Analytics. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Regulatory Compliance is outside the competency scope of the Director of Analytics. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
